name: Friends of KDSP
mission: Friends of KDSP supports the empowerment of the Down syndrome community in Pakistan by providing quality developmental services to individuals with Down syndrome and promoting and advocating for their inclusion in mainstream society.
theoryOfChange: Supports empowerment of the Down syndrome community in Pakistan by providing quality developmental services and promoting/advocating for inclusion in mainstream society; vision is one-stop access to quality affordable developmental services enabling independence and contribution to society.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2022 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 36% of total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: High fundraising spend reduces funds available for programs.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 23/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Fundraising costs are 36% of total expenses

risks.description: Fundraising costs are 36% of total expenses

score_details.score_summary: Friends of Kdsp shows moderate impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied). Evidence score may reflect limited reporting capacity, not program weakness.

all_citations.claim: Friends of Kdsp reports a program expense ratio of 60.4% and fundraising efficiency of $0.23 per $1 raised.
The organization is eligible for sadaqah donations.
Total revenue for the organization is $848,904 with 28.5 months of working capital.
The charity facilitates surgeries and mainstream school integration for the Down syndrome community in Pakistan.
Donation options are available for general charitable giving and support of organizational programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate overall score due to its specialized mission and strong working capital, offset by a lower program expense ratio and limited third-party data verification.
areas_for_improvement: Lower program expense ratio of 60.4% compared to high-performing benchmarks
Limited data transparency resulting in low data confidence scores

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated at 23/50, reflecting a balanced fit for donors interested in global education and disability advocacy [4]. As a sadaqah-eligible organization, it provides a pathway for general charitable giving toward urgent developmental causes in Pakistan [2][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The charity demonstrates a moderate impact score of 26/50, focusing on capacity building through facilitated surgeries and medical procedures [4]. While it maintains a total revenue of $848,904, its program expense ratio is 60.4%, and it operates with a fundraising efficiency of $0.23 per $1 raised [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Friends of Kdsp empowers the Down syndrome community in Pakistan through specialized healthcare, early intervention, and educational integration.
strengths: Strong liquidity with 28.5 months of working capital available to sustain operations
Focused impact on capacity building through medical procedures and mainstream school integration
baseline_narrative.summary: Friends of Kdsp is a non-profit organization dedicated to supporting individuals with Down syndrome in Pakistan by providing essential services and fostering an inclusive society [4]. The organization operates through key initiatives including Program 1, Sehat Healthcare, and Early Childhood Intervention to ensure comprehensive developmental support [1][4].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate overall score due to its specialized mission and strong working capital, offset by a lower program expense ratio and limited third-party data verification.

areas_for_improvement.context: The charity reports a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 60.4%</cite>, which is lower than the 75% benchmark typically seen in high-performing humanitarian organizations.
It costs the organization <cite id="1">$0.29 to raise every $1</cite>, indicating a need for more efficient donor acquisition strategies.

bbb_assessment.summary: Recognized as a BBB Accredited Charity according to external discovery data.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's high reserves mitigate the risk of service disruption during economic downturns.
risk_factors: <cite id="1">Fundraising efficiency of $0.29/$1 is higher than average</cite>
<cite id="3">Limited board size may restrict governance oversight</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="1">high overhead costs</cite> and a lack of third-party impact evaluations.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023, Impact data from 2024
confidence.notes: Low confidence due to limited transparency and small board size.

alignment.explanation: The charity is a strong fit for donors interested in <cite id="4">disability rights and education in Pakistan</cite>. It is currently <cite id="2">Sadaqah-eligible</cite>.

credibility.explanation: The organization demonstrates financial responsibility through <cite id="3">clean tax filings</cite> and a <cite id="1">very high working capital ratio</cite>. However, credibility is limited by <cite id="3">low data confidence</cite> and a small board of only <cite id="3">three members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To improve credibility, the charity should <cite id="1">publish audited financial statements</cite> and expand its board to include more independent oversight.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by direct capacity building, with a <cite id="4">cost per beneficiary of approximately $125.79</cite>. They have successfully integrated <cite id="4">80% of their students</cite> into mainstream schools.
impact.improvement: The charity could improve impact by <cite id="1">increasing its program expense ratio</cite> from 60.4% toward the sector standard of 75%.

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Funds specialized healthcare and educational services through local entities.

rich_narrative.headline: Friends of Kdsp empowers the Down syndrome community in Pakistan through specialized healthcare, early intervention, and educational integration.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support long-term disability inclusion and educational empowerment for children in Pakistan through a financially stable institution.
donor_motivations: Value long-term social integration over short-term relief
Care about the rights and health of the Down syndrome community
Prefer organizations with a deep, specialized focus in one region
Seek to provide Sadaqah Jariyah through educational capacity building
giving_considerations: Donations support a specialized niche that is often overlooked
The organization has a very long financial runway, ensuring stability
Current fundraising costs are higher than some larger peers

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects consistent self-reporting of outcomes like school integration and medical procedures, but lacks independent evaluation.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: By providing early intervention and healthcare, the charity enables children with Down syndrome to enter mainstream schools and eventually the workforce.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking integration rates ensures that the therapy provided actually leads to social inclusion.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The organization is in a growth phase and seeks to expand its inclusive education model across more regions in Pakistan.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="4">80% mainstream school integration rate</cite> and <cite id="1">25-month funding runway</cite>.
peer_comparison.peer_group: Disability Support Organizations in South Asia

strengths.detail: The charity maintains <cite id="1">28.5 months of working capital</cite> in reserve, providing a massive safety net for its Pakistan-based operations.
The organization has achieved an <cite id="4">80% mainstream school integration rate</cite>, moving children with Down syndrome into standard classrooms.
Programs span the entire developmental lifecycle, from <cite id="4">Early Childhood Intervention</cite> to <cite id="4">Skills Development</cite> for adults.

strengths_deep_dive: Friends of Kdsp excels in creating tangible pathways for social inclusion. Their healthcare initiative, Sehat, has successfully <cite id="4">facilitated 400 medical procedures</cite>, addressing the specific physiological needs of the Down syndrome community. Beyond health, the organization acts as a bridge to the formal education system. By providing <cite id="4">weekly therapy sessions</cite> and specialized preschool training, they prepare students for the rigors of mainstream schooling. This approach is backed by significant financial stability, as evidenced by their <cite id="3">$461,455 in total assets</cite> against minimal <cite id="3">liabilities of only $528</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Friends of Kdsp is a specialized non-profit dedicated to <cite id="4">empowering the Down syndrome community in Pakistan</cite>. The organization addresses critical gaps in developmental support through a multi-pronged approach that includes <cite id="4">healthcare, early intervention, and inclusive education</cite>. By focusing on capacity building, the charity helps individuals lead independent lives. Their operations are characterized by <cite id="1">strong liquidity</cite>, maintaining <cite id="1">28.5 months of working capital</cite> to ensure service continuity in a volatile economic environment. In 2023, the organization reported <cite id="3">$655,185 in total revenue</cite>, which is primarily driven by <cite id="3">individual contributions</cite>. While the organization is relatively young, it has already demonstrated significant reach, facilitating <cite id="4">400 surgeries and medical procedures</cite> for its beneficiaries. Their model emphasizes long-term integration, achieving an <cite id="4">80% mainstream school integration rate</cite> for children in their programs. This focus on depth of service ensures that beneficiaries receive sustained support from early childhood through skill development.
